planner.summary: Root cause: MainActivity.kt lines 419-420 send the full markdown content directly to LlmChatModelHelper.sendMessage() instead of a brief notification. The PDF is correctly saved to workspace, but the entire extracted text is injected into the chat as a user message.
planner.problem_statement: When a user attaches a PDF, the entire markdown-converted content is being injected directly into the chat instead of a single brief notification message like 'Document processed and saved to workspace'.

evidence.detail: LlmChatModelHelper.sendMessage(message = markdown, ...) — the full extracted markdown is sent as the user message. The markdown variable at this point contains the complete PDF-to-markdown conversion result (potentially thousands of characters).
PdfToMarkdownConverter converts the PDF to markdown, returning the full text content which is then assigned to the `markdown` variable used at line 420.
The PDF and markdown ARE correctly saved to WorkspaceManager (pdfPath, mdPath), so the document is available in workspace. The bug is that the content is ALSO sent to the LLM as a message.

acceptance_criteria: When a PDF is attached, the chat shows a single bot message with a brief notification (e.g., 'Document X.pdf processed and saved to workspace') instead of the full markdown content
The PDF and markdown file are still correctly saved to WorkspaceManager (regression check)
The model can still access the markdown file via tool calling (e.g., /read command) — workspace functionality is preserved
risks: The markdown content was intentionally sent to the LLM to have the model summarize or process the document. Changing to a brief notification means the model won't automatically process/summarize — user would need to explicitly ask. Verify this is the desired behavior.
test_plan: 1. Attach a PDF via the paperclip button
2. Observe: bot message should show brief notification like 'Document X.pdf processed (N characters) — saved to workspace', NOT the full markdown content
3. Verify: PDF file exists in workspace/documents/, markdown file exists in workspace/markdown/
4. Verify: /read command can still retrieve the markdown content from workspace

behavioral_verification.symptom: PDF attachment causes full markdown text to appear in chat instead of brief notification
behavioral_verification.required_observation: After attaching a PDF, the bot message contains only a brief notification (e.g., 'Document X.pdf processed and saved to workspace') and NOT the full extracted markdown text
evidence_required: MainActivity.kt line 419-420 changes message=markdown to message='Document X.pdf processed and saved to workspace' or similar brief text

 This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
 
-## Build & Test
+## Project Overview
 
-```bash
-# Clean build (Windows/OneDrive: delete app/build first if corrupted)
-.\build.bat
+Gemma4Android is an Android chat application for on-device LLM inference using **Gemma-4-E2B-IT** with Google's **LiteRT-LM** (GPU/OpenCL acceleration, experimental NPU fallback). Target: Android 15 (SDK 35) on devices like Nubia Flip 5G.
 
-# Install APK
-adb install -r app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk
-```
+## Build & Run
 
-## Integration Tests (NPU)
-
-Use the shell script for full end-to-end tests:
-
-```bash
-# NPU direct backend test
-./test_npu_direct.sh
-```
-
-Each script:
-1. Clears logs
-2. Installs test APK
-3. Runs UiAutomator test via am instrument
-4. Verifies result (✅/❌)
-5. Shows relevant logcat output
-
-See `test_npu_direct.sh` source for details.
-
-## Test Harness (unit tests only)
-
-For on-device unit tests, add to `TestHarnessActivity.runTests()`:
-
-## Standard Test Workflow
-
-For every change, follow this sequence:
-
-```
-1. [Understand] Understand the task fully before writing code
-2. [Diff] Show the diff on screen — minimal change only
-3. [Build] .\build.bat — clean build, no cache issues
-4. [Install] adb install -r app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk
-5. [Verify] Return layout JSON (adb shell dumpsys activity activities) + logcat
-```
-
-```bash
-# Full verification command
-.\build.bat && adb install -r app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk && adb shell dumpsys activity activities && adb logcat -d
-```
-
-## Todo List
-
-For every user request, create a todo list tracking each step:
-
-```
-- [ ] Understand the task
-- [ ] Write minimal code change
-- [ ] Show diff for review
-- [ ] Build with .\build.bat
-- [ ] Install via ADB
-- [ ] Return layout JSON + logcat
-```
+- **Build APK:** `./gradlew assembleDebug` → `app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`
+- **Install:** `adb install -r app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`
+- **Start:** `adb shell am start -n com.gemma.gpuchat/.MainActivity`
+- **Unit tests:** `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest`
+- **Instrumentation tests:** `./gradlew connectedDebugAndroidTest`
+- **NPU validation:** `./test_npu_direct.sh` (requires connected device with model files)
+- **Logs:** `adb logcat -s GEMMA LlmChatModelHelper LiteRT`
 
 ## Architecture
 
-Android chat app using on-device AI with Jetpack Compose + Material3.
-
-```
-app/src/main/java/com/gemma/gpuchat/
-├── MainActivity.kt         # Compose UI + audio recording
-├── LlmChatModelHelper.kt   # LiteRT-LM engine wrapper (singleton)
-├── AgentTools.kt           # ToolSet: workspace, calendar, location
-├── LlmPreferences.kt        # DataStore-backed settings
-├── WorkspaceManager.kt      # PDF storage + markdown conversion
-├── ChatHistoryManager.kt    # Conversation persistence
-├── AudioTranscriber.kt     # Voice-to-text (separate LLM instance)
-├── AppLogger.kt            # File-based logging
-└── TestHarnessActivity.kt  # On-device unit test runner
-```
-
-## LiteRT-LM Integration
-
-- **Model:** `/data/local/tmp/gemma-4-E2B-it.litertlm` (2.58GB), GPU-first
-- **Tool calling:** Manual mode (`automaticToolCalling = false`) — app reflects on `AgentTools`
-- **Thinking:** `Channel("thought", ...)` + `extraContext: {enable_thinking: true}`
-- **System instruction:** Prepend to user message (model ignores `ConversationConfig.systemInstruction`)
-
-## Key Gotchas
-
-- WAV audio: must have `fact` chunk (56-byte header) — raw PCM causes crash error -10
-- UI thread: LiteRT-LM callbacks run on background threads — use `mainHandler.post {}`
-- Conversation reset: `conversation.close()` then `engine.createConversation()` (no reset API)
-- First inference after restart may return 0 chars — restart app and retry
-- `onSettingsChange` must NO
... truncated ...
```
test_strategy_markdown:
## Test Strategy: estrategia_teste.md

# Estratégia de Teste - Gemma4Android

## Níveis de Teste

### 1. Teste de Build (verificação estática)
- `./gradlew assembleDebug` - compilação do APK
- Verifica se não há erros de compilação Kotlin/Compose
- **Evidência**: `app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` gerado com sucesso

### 2. Teste Unitário
- `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest` - testes unitários Java/Kotlin
- **Evidência**: relatório em `app/build/reports/tests/testDebugUnitTest/index.html`

### 3. Teste de Instrumentação (dispositivo real)
- `./gradlew connectedDebugAndroidTest` - requer dispositivo/emulador conectado
- **Comando**: `adb install -r app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`
- **Esperado**: dispositivo com Android 15 (SDK 35)

### 4. Teste Manual - PDF Chat Flow (ui.py)

**Sintoma verificado**: Ao enviar PDF, não deve haver mensagens "travadas" ou duplicadas no início.

**Roteiro de teste usando ui.py:**

```bash
# Adb está no diretório do projeto
export PATH="$PWD:$PATH"

# 1. Build e instalação
./gradlew assembleDebug
./adb install -r app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk

# 2. Iniciar app e aguardar init do modelo
python ui.py start
# Aguardar ~35s até "Pronto!" aparecer

# 3. dump inicial para verificar estado
python ui.py dump

# 4. Clicar no botão de anexar PDF (AttachFile)
python ui.py tap --desc "Attach PDF"

# 5. Capturar estado durante processamento
python ui.py screenshot --filename /tmp/pdf_step1_init.png
python ui.py dump > /tmp/pdf_dump1.txt

sleep 5
python ui.py screenshot --filename /tmp/pdf_step2_thinking.png
python ui.py dump > /tmp/pdf_dump2.txt

sleep 10
python ui.py screenshot --filename /tmp/pdf_step3_response.png
python ui.py dump > /tmp/pdf_dump3.txt

# 6. Verificar logs
./adb logcat -s GEMMA MainActivity LlmChatModelHelper > /tmp/pdf_logcat.txt

# 7. Verificações esperadas:
# - Dump 1: 1 mensagem de usuário (PDF), 1 mensagem de bot (vazia) = 2 mensagens
# - Dump 2: Thinking bubble visível, thinkingText não vazio
# - Dump 3: Thinking bubble escondida (isThinkingDone=true), resposta final na bot message
# - Log: [THINKING-UI] callbacks aparecem progressivamente
```

**Passos para verificar (observação visual)**:
1. ✅ `adb install -r app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`
2. ✅ `adb shell am start -n com.gemma.gpuchat/.MainActivity`
3. ✅ Anexar PDF via botão de atachamento (📎)
4. ✅ Observar o chat:
   - ✅ Deve aparecer apenas uma mensagem do bot (vazia) aguardando resposta
   - ✅ Thinking deve aparecer em sequência (`ThinkingBubble`)
   - ✅ Resposta final deve chegar após thinking
   - ❌ NÃO deve haver mensagem "📄 Processando PDF..." antes
   - ❌ NÃO deve haver notificação de workspace como mensagem separada

**Evidência requerida**:
- Log do adb: `adb logcat -s GEMMA MainActivity` durante o fluxo PDF
- Screenshot do chat após resposta completa
- Contagem de mensagens nos dumps (deve ser 2: 1 user + 1 bot)

## Quando verificação estática é insuficiente

- Build passando não garante que o fluxo de UI (PDF → markdown → thinking → resposta) funciona corretamente
- Teste manual em dispositivo real é obrigatório para verificar timing das mensagens e callbacks de streaming
- Logs de `LlmChatModelHelper` são essenciais para confirmar que não há mensagens duplicadas no início

## Correção Aplicada (v0.2)

**Problema**: Duas mensagens "travadas" no início do PDF — a loading message "📄 Processando PDF..." e workspace notification messages criavam mensagens extras antes do thinking.

**Solução**:
1. Removida loading message "📄 Processando PDF..."
2. Removidas workspace notification messages que criavam mensagens duplicadas
3. Enviado `markdown` content diretamente ao modelo (não mais uma notificação)
4. Corrigido `AnimatedVisibility` para esconder `ThinkingBubble` quando `isThinkingDone=true`
